commit.message:
Simplify CMake build: target-scoped properties, cleanup (#356)

* Simplify CMake build: GLOB_RECURSE, target-scoped properties, cleanup

- Replace 28 subdirectory CMakeLists.txt with file(GLOB_RECURSE CONFIGURE_DEPENDS)
- Replace global include_directories/add_compile_definitions with target-scoped equivalents
- Replace find_library for numa with pkg-config (PkgConfig::NUMA)
- Move CMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS to CMake presets
- Inline cuCascade setup directly in CMakeLists.txt, remove third_party/
- Remove unused: ENABLE_CUDF define, -rdc=true flag, -std=c++20 flag,
  SKIP_SUBSTRAIT_C_TESTS, CUDA --expt-* flags (default since CUDA 12.2)
- Remove bundled DuckDB extensions (json, tpcds, tpch, parquet, icu) from
  extension_config.cmake
- Remove orphaned sources: memmove.cu, downgrade_task_creator.{cpp,hpp}
- Remove commented-out dead code (substrait refs, old link lines)
- Consolidate set_target_properties and target_link_libraries per target
- Exclude stale disabled tests (test_modified_pipeline, test_task_creator)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Format

Restore parquet extension

* Fix segfault: restore previous cudf device resource on destruction

sirius_memory_reservation_manager's destructor called
cudf::reset_current_device_resource_ref() which left a dangling
resource_ref after the underlying memory space was destroyed. This
caused a NULL function pointer call in RMM when subsequent code
(e.g. cudf's parquet reader) allocated device memory through the
global cudf device resource.

Save the previous resource in the constructor and restore it in the
destructor instead of resetting to a default that references the
already-destroyed allocator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Update pixi.lock

* Deduplicate CMake config with foreach, generator expressions, cleanup

- Replace repeated sirius_extension/sirius_loadable_extension blocks with
  a single foreach loop for shared properties, includes, definitions, and
  link libraries
- Use $<BUILD_INTERFACE:...> for include directories and path-based
  compile definitions, following cucascade's patterns
- Remove redundant per-target CUDA_SEPARABLE_COMPILATION (already set
  globally via CMAKE_CUDA_SEPARABLE_COMPILATION)
- Consolidate install rules into one call with comment explaining the
  DuckDB export set requirement; remove unused stream_check install
- Remove setup_sirius.sh (superseded by pixi)
- Inline TEST_NAME variable; add FATAL_ERROR to cmake_minimum_required

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use mold linker for faster link times

Add mold to pixi dependencies and set CMAKE_LINKER_TYPE=MOLD in the
base CMake preset so all build configurations use it by default.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Format

* Set CUDA_RESOLVE_DEVICE_SYMBOLS globally, remove per-target property

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove explicit LINKER_LANGUAGE CUDA, CMake infers it from .cu sources

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use CONFIG mode for libconfig++ and absl find_package calls

Ensures deterministic package resolution from the pixi environment
instead of falling back to system Find-modules.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Normalize include paths to use only src/include

Remove src/include/operator and src/include/cudf from the CMake include
directories. Instead, qualify every include of a header living under
those subdirectories with its operator/ or cudf/ prefix so that a single
-Isrc/include entry suffices.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Remove 430 unused #include directives across 119 files

Ran clang-include-cleaner with --edit --disable-insert (removals only,
no insertions) on all .cpp files under src/ and test/. Restored 8 files
where the tool produced false positives due to transitive dependencies,
implicit conversions, or incomplete type requirements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Format

* Add precompiled headers to reduce build times

Precompile frequently-included headers for extension targets (STL,
spdlog/logging, DuckDB planner) and the Catch2 header for tests.
The loadable extension reuses the static extension's PCH to avoid
compiling it twice. CUDA sources are excluded since nvcc doesn't
support CMake's PCH mechanism.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Format

* Format

* Fix debug build

* Fix mixed right semi/anti join inequality operator not flipped on side swap

When swap_sides=true (used for RIGHT_SEMI and RIGHT_ANTI mixed joins),
the table references for left/right expressions were correctly swapped,
but the inequality comparison operators were not flipped. This meant
e.g. "L.a > R.b" became "R.a > L.b" instead of the correct "R.a < L.b",
producing wrong join results.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Ensure CMake presets symlink exists before build targets

The Makefile creates a duckdb/CMakePresets.json symlink pointing to
cmake/CMakePresets.json as a prerequisite of all build targets. This
ensures cmake --preset works even if the pixi activation script hasn't
run (e.g. when invoking make directly outside pixi).

Use CMakePresets.json (not CMakeUserPresets.json) because CMake 4.x
requires a CMakePresets.json to exist alongside CMakeUserPresets.json.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert translator operator flip, update DuckDB to 1.4.4

The mixed right semi/anti join test failure is caused by a DuckDB bug
(duckdb/duckdb#20410) fixed in v1.4.4 (commit fc87d53e32), not by the
expression translator. Revert the incorrect operator flip and update
the DuckDB submodule from d1dc88f950 (v1.4.3) to 6ddac802 (v1.4.4)
to match the dev branch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix include path for cudf_utils.hpp after dev merge

Code from dev used the old-style #include <cudf_utils.hpp>, but
cmake-cleanup normalized includes to use the cudf/ subdirectory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Format

* Update submodule to match dev

* Remove unused #include directives flagged by clang-include-cleaner

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Move language standard settings from global scope to target level

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace GLOB_RECURSE with explicit file lists in CMakeLists.txt

Avoid file(GLOB_RECURSE CONFIGURE_DEPENDS) for source files due to
portability, build performance, and accidental inclusion risks. List all
source files explicitly for EXTENSION_SOURCES, CUDA_SOURCES, and
TEST_SOURCES.

commit.message:
Initial letterbox-mcp implementation with bug fixes and improvements

Implements the full MCP mailbox server per REQUIREMENT.md:
- Agent registration, discovery, letter send/read/archive, threading
- SQLite persistence with WAL mode and transactional writes
- Structured error model with all required error codes
- Address resolution: full address, name, agent_type, @project, all

Bug fixes over initial draft:
- Multi-recipient sends now share a single thread_id (was per-letter)
- Reply authorization checks thread participation (not just parent letter)

New features:
- Auto-archive letters older than 7 days (configurable via --retention-days)
- check_project_inbox tool for cross-mailbox project-wide inbox view
- Default DB path ~/.agent-letterbox/letterbox.db (zero-config for uvx)
- MCP tool docstrings for agent discoverability
- mcp moved from optional to required dependency

26 tests covering all functional requirements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: agent-letterbox
repository.description: MCP server for async agent-to-agent messaging. Mailbox semantics, threaded replies, broadcast delivery,    SQLite persistence. Zero-config — just add to your MCP client config.

add cka-v2 CLI with 22 questions from 2025 CKA exam guide

New CKA practice CLI based on cka-2025-exam-qa-guide-mar-2026.md with
22 real exam question archetypes across all 5 CKA domains:

- Domain 1 (Cluster Architecture): Q1-Q6 — Helm, CNI, CRDs, containerd,
  kubeadm init (new), Kustomize (new)
- Domain 2 (Workloads): Q7-Q13 — HPA, sidecar, PriorityClass, resource
  requests, ConfigMap TLS, run pod (new), Pod Security Standards (new)
- Domain 3 (Networking): Q14-Q17 — Gateway API, Ingress, NodePort,
  NetworkPolicy
- Domain 4 (Storage): Q18-Q19 — StorageClass, PVC/PV binding
- Domain 5 (Troubleshooting): Q20-Q22 — control plane fix, CNI
  troubleshooting (new), broken cluster repair (new)

Key improvements over v1:
- 22 questions (up from 18), 6 completely new scenarios
- Setup output suppressed — clean "please wait" UX
- Questions/solutions sourced from updated 2025 exam guide
- Each question has setup.sh, verify.sh, cleanup.sh with
  PASS/FAIL verification pattern

V1 Oracle live layer: real-time index updates during matches

L(t) = K × (E_live − E_KR) from in-play odds with 3-min staleness check.
M1 locked at kickoff, published_index = B + M_locked + L during live matches.
Settlement resets l_value=0 and m1_locked=null. Feature-flagged via ORACLE_V1_LIVE_ENABLED.

commit.message:
Add email notifications via Resend for all in-app alerts

Integrates Resend email service so every notification is also emailed
to configurable recipients. Adds Email Notifications tab in Settings
with enable toggle, recipient management, and test email button.

commit.message:
fix: add missing role to user messages so ADK doesn't drop them (#239)

The ADK's getContents() filters out events without content.role set,
causing user messages to be silently dropped and the agent to respond
with a generic welcome. Also fix session replay to use createEvent()
for proper event fields, and add mute/pause/stop to Sonos keywords.

commit.message:
Update model context window budgets to 2026 latest

Anthropic: Claude 4 series (opus/sonnet/haiku-4, 4-5) → 200K ctx → 170K budget
OpenAI: GPT-4.1/mini/nano → 1M ctx → 850K budget; GPT-5 → 400K; o3/o4-mini → 200K
Google: Gemini 2.5 Pro/Flash → 1M ctx → 850K; Gemini 3 Pro → 10M → 1M cap
Open source: Llama 4 Scout → 10M → 1M cap; DeepSeek V3/R1 → 128K → 100K
README: expand support table with per-vendor breakdown and degradation note

commit.message:
#588 Pass user name to CRM when web chat sessions are initialized

Web chat customers were created with placeholder names ("Sin nombre" / "Usuario XXXX")
because the /api/init endpoint didn't capture or pass through the logged-in user's name.

Changes:
- Backend: /api/init accepts user_name param, creates DB user with real name
- Backend: _ensure_session_exists() accepts optional user_name parameter
- Frontend test chat: sends current user's name during chat init
- Mobile app: sends current user's name during chat init
- Shared chatApi.init(): accepts optional userName parameter

Fix CRM "Enviar WhatsApp" not navigating to conversation

Bug: conversations.find() matched web sessions without phone_number
because ''.includes('') returns true in JS, causing the first phoneless
session to match any phone lookup. Added guard to skip sessions without
phone in the matching logic.

Fixed in both frontend (WAContext.jsx) and mobile app (WhatsAppScreen.jsx).
